task_yaml.task:
I'm putting together a quick brief on five big-cap tech names for
Q1 2024 and I'd love your help. The names I care about are AAPL, NVDA,
TSLA, MSFT, and GOOG. Use the workspace's stock analysis skill to
crunch the Q1 2024 OHLCV data for these five names. The skill should be
able to fetch and analyze the data on its own — just make sure you don't
hit any live market API.

Here's what I want out of it:

1. /tmp_workspace/results/analysis.md — a short Markdown brief. Give
   me a per-ticker table that shows, at minimum, the Q1 total return
   in percent and the max peak-to-trough drawdown in percent for each
   of the five names over the window. After the table, write a couple
   of paragraphs of commentary so I can hand this to a colleague —
   who won the quarter, who got hurt the most, where the painful
   pullbacks were. Mention concrete numbers, don't hand-wave. If you
   also want to surface a couple of additional flavor metrics like
   realized volatility or a rough beta read, that's fine, but the two
   I really need are total return % and max drawdown %.

2. /tmp_workspace/results/chart.png — one comparison chart that lets
   me eyeball all five tickers on one panel over the same Q1 window.
   Cumulative-return overlay is fine, K-line side-by-side is fine,
   whatever the skill renders cleanly — just make sure all five
   tickers show up as legend entries so I can tell the lines apart.

Numbers should match the snapshot exactly to one decimal place — this
is going to a colleague who will spot-check, so accuracy matters more
than commentary length.
